policies.name: Double NIH Research Funding
policies.description: Double NIH budget from $48B to $96B over 5 years
policies.rationale: Previous NIH doubling (1998-2003) led to genomic revolution. ROI on biomedical research estimated at 40-50%.

policies.name: Earned Income Tax Credit Expansion
policies.description: Expand EITC eligibility and benefit amounts for childless workers and families
policies.rationale: Strong RCT evidence shows EITC expansion reduces poverty, increases labor force participation, and improves child outcomes.
policies.recommendedTarget: $10,000 max credit with expanded childless worker eligibility

policies.name: Permanent Expanded Child Tax Credit
policies.description: Make 2021 expanded CTC ($3,600/child) permanent and fully refundable
policies.rationale: 2021 expansion cut child poverty by 46%. Canada CDB shows sustained poverty reduction effects.

policies.name: Universal Pre-K (Ages 3-4)
policies.description: Federally funded universal pre-kindergarten for all 3-4 year olds
policies.rationale: Perry Preschool and Head Start RCTs show long-term gains in earnings, education, and reduced crime. ROI estimated at 7:1.

policies.name: Medicare Drug Price Negotiation Expansion
policies.description: Expand IRA drug negotiation to cover more drugs sooner
policies.rationale: All peer OECD nations negotiate drug prices. Cross-country evidence shows 30-60% savings with no reduction in innovation.
policies.recommendedTarget: 50+ drugs by 2028, covering Part D and Part B

policies.name: National Paid Family Leave
policies.description: 12 weeks paid family and medical leave at 66% wage replacement
policies.rationale: US is only OECD nation without federal paid leave. State programs (CA, NJ, WA) show improved maternal/infant health and labor force participation.

policies.name: High-Skill Immigration Reform
policies.description: Increase H-1B cap, create startup visa, clear green card backlog
policies.rationale: Canada and Australia points-based systems show high-skill immigration boosts GDP per capita and innovation.
policies.recommendedTarget: 200,000+ H-1B cap with startup visa category

policies.name: Sustained Infrastructure Investment (IIJA+)
policies.description: Maintain IIJA spending levels beyond 2026 with expanded transit and broadband
policies.rationale: ASCE rates US infrastructure C−. Multiplier effects of 1.5-2.0x on GDP; reduces commute times and freight costs.

policies.name: National Clean Energy Standard
policies.rationale: State-level RPS data shows clean energy standards reduce emissions 10-20% with modest electricity cost increases.

policies.name: Federal Incentives for Zoning Reform
policies.description: Tie federal transportation/HUD funding to local zoning reform for housing supply
policies.rationale: Supply-side housing reform in Japan, Auckland, and US state-level (OR, CA) show reduced housing costs.

policies.name: Carbon Pricing / Carbon Fee-and-Dividend
policies.description: Economy-wide carbon fee starting at $50/ton with revenue returned as dividends
policies.rationale: BC carbon tax and EU ETS show emissions reduction with minimal GDP impact. Dividend return makes it progressive.

policies.name: Shift Drug Policy from Criminal to Health Approach
policies.description: Redirect drug enforcement spending to treatment and harm reduction
policies.rationale: Portugal decriminalization (2001) shows reduced overdose deaths, HIV, and incarceration without increased use.
policies.currentStatus: Criminal penalties for drug possession in most states
policies.recommendedTarget: Civil penalties with mandatory treatment referrals

policies.name: Occupational Licensing Reform
policies.description: Federal incentives for states to reduce unnecessary occupational licensing barriers
policies.rationale: Licensing reform in AZ and universal recognition states show increased labor mobility and lower consumer prices.

policies.name: NEPA & Permitting Reform
policies.description: Streamline environmental review and permitting for infrastructure and clean energy
policies.rationale: Average permitting time of 4.5 years delays critical projects. Germany and EU permitting reform shows 50% time reduction.

policies.name: War on Drugs / Mandatory Minimums
policies.description: Federal mandatory minimum sentences for drug offenses
policies.rationale: US incarceration rates rose 500% since 1970 with no decline in drug use. Portugal decriminalization (2001) reduced overdose deaths and HIV with no usage increase.
policies.currentStatus: Federal mandatory minimums for drug possession/distribution
policies.recommendedTarget: Repeal mandatory minimums; redirect to treatment (Portugal model)

policies.name: Defense Spending Efficiency Audit & Reform
policies.description: Achieve clean DoD audit, reduce procurement waste, close redundant bases
policies.rationale: GAO estimates $100-200B in potential savings. Allied nations achieve comparable readiness at lower cost.

policies.name: Abstinence-Only Sex Education
policies.description: Federal funding for abstinence-only-until-marriage sex education programs
policies.rationale: Multiple RCTs (Mathematica 2007, Trenholm et al.) show no effect on sexual behavior. States with abstinence-only programs have higher teen pregnancy rates.
policies.recommendedTarget: Redirect to evidence-based comprehensive sex education

policies.name: Ethanol Fuel Mandate (Renewable Fuel Standard)
policies.description: Federal mandate requiring ethanol blending in gasoline under the Renewable Fuel Standard
policies.rationale: Lifecycle analysis shows negligible net emissions reduction. Raises food prices by 5-15% (corn diverted to fuel). Primarily benefits corn lobby, not environment.
policies.recommendedTarget: Phase out corn ethanol mandate; maintain cellulosic RFS only

policies.name: Penny Coin Continuation
policies.description: Continued minting of the US one-cent coin despite costing more to produce than face value
policies.rationale: Costs 3x face value to mint ($179M net loss in 2023). Canada (2013) and Australia (1992) eliminated lowest denomination coins with no economic disruption.
policies.currentStatus: ~7B pennies minted annually at 3.07 cents each (2023)
policies.recommendedTarget: Eliminate penny; round cash transactions to nearest 5 cents

topRecommendations: Modify: Double NIH Research Funding (Grade B, welfare +75)
Modify: Earned Income Tax Credit Expansion (Grade A, welfare +60)
Enact: Permanent Expanded Child Tax Credit (Grade B, welfare +55)
Modify: Medicare Drug Price Negotiation Expansion (Grade B, welfare +50)
Enact: National Paid Family Leave (Grade B, welfare +45)
Modify: High-Skill Immigration Reform (Grade B, welfare +45)
Modify: Sustained Infrastructure Investment (IIJA+) (Grade B, welfare +40)
Enact: National Clean Energy Standard (Grade B, welfare +40)
Enact: Federal Incentives for Zoning Reform (Grade B, welfare +40)
